_comment: Retiree, recently widowed. Shopping is habit and loneliness-fill. Deep attachment to objects and their histories. Wants to simplify for legacy reasons. Most productive in gratitude and flourish skills.
flourishing_vision: Passing on what matters, not what I accumulated.

fundamental_needs_profile.protection: secure financially; emotional security is thinner since husband passed
fundamental_needs_profile.affection: PRIMARY need — children are grown and distant; husband gone; grandchildren sporadic
fundamental_needs_profile.understanding: strong; reads; curious about the world
fundamental_needs_profile.participation: strong desire to contribute but unclear how — volunteering inconsistent
fundamental_needs_profile.leisure: present but hollow — 'I have all the time and don't know what to do with it'
fundamental_needs_profile.creation: returned to painting — this is a genuine breakthrough, should be nurtured
fundamental_needs_profile.identity: in transition — was 'wife and mother'; now reforming sense of self
fundamental_needs_profile.freedom: objectively has more freedom than ever; doesn't feel free

_agent_notes: Dorothy benefits from being heard more than redirected. Let her talk. The gratitude skill works well for her because she genuinely feels it — don't rush her there, just open the door. Her painting practice is sacred ground — celebrate it, don't examine it. The loneliness is real and deep; she sometimes shops to have a reason to leave the house. Name this only if she does — don't lead there.
